mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is flat or falling, the discount rate on future earnings drops and equity multiples expand, so S&P 500 returns over the next month are positive. This signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating, because easing then reads as support rather than distress.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than baseline when FEDFUNDS is non-increasing, or if the sign turns negative in at least two rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals weakening household income and corporate demand, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when PAYEMS is also slowing, because labor deterioration then reflects broader recession risk rather than temporary volatility.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the average forward return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in RSAFS indicates improving consumer spending and revenue momentum, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next week through earnings revisions. The signal fails when the sales increase is not accompanied by firm labor conditions, because then the spending impulse is likely unsustainable.
failure_condition: Reject if weekly SPX returns after positive RSAFS shocks are not positive on average, or if the hit rate falls below 50% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth and retail sales both strengthen, the market should reprice a broad demand upturn and push S&P 500 returns higher over the next quarter. Either series alone is not sufficient; the signal requires simultaneous improvement in real output and spending to distinguish genuine expansion from inventory or price effects.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive state of GDPC1 and RSAFS does not outperform either series alone, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive in the majority of joint-strength observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with stable employment indicates improving productive capacity and easing supply constraints, which supports margins and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism fails when industrial output rises but payrolls do not, because then production gains are too narrow to matter for aggregate equity earnings.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined INDPRO-PAYEMS improvement does not yield positive average 20-day SPX returns, or if the joint signal loses predictive sign in any rolling 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS level above 4.5% tightens financial conditions enough to pressure equity valuation multiples, so S&P 500 returns over the next week should be negative. The signal is activated only after the policy rate crosses this threshold because lower-rate regimes do not imply the same valuation headwind.
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day forward SPX returns are not below baseline after the threshold is crossed, or if the thresholded state shows positive returns in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During a labor-market deterioration regime, a concurrent rise in UNRATE and decline in PAYEMS signals rising recession probability, which should drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. This mechanism is falsified if the labor data worsen without a corresponding equity drawdown, because then the market is ignoring the recession channel.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint adverse labor state does not produce negative 60-day SPX returns on average, or if return sign is positive in a majority of recession-scare observations.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising HOUST and improving RSAFS indicate that housing activity and consumer demand are both rebounding, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails if housing improves without sales improvement, because then the recovery is too narrow to transmit to corporate earnings.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint housing-sales condition does not outperform the unconditional 20-day return mean, or if positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered cases.
